market_viability.1: No identifiable market. Pure speculation with no supporting data.
market_viability.3: Small or unclear market. Weak demand signals, no competitor validation.
market_viability.5: Market exists but size uncertain. Some evidence of demand, few competitors.
market_viability.7: Clear market with evidence. Known competitors validate demand. Growth signals present.
market_viability.9: Large, growing market with strong demand signals. Multiple successful competitors.
market_viability.10: Proven market with direct user validation data and measurable willingness to pay.

user_desirability.1: No evidence users want this. Solving a non-problem.
user_desirability.3: Weak user signal. Pain exists but alternatives are good enough.
user_desirability.5: Users have the pain but current solutions partially address it.
user_desirability.7: Strong pain point with clear gaps in current solutions. Users actively seeking alternatives.
user_desirability.9: Acute pain. Users hacking workarounds. High willingness to switch.
user_desirability.10: Users already paying for inferior solutions and actively requesting this.

technical_feasibility.1: Impossible with current stack. Requires fundamental pivot or unproven technology.
technical_feasibility.3: Major unknowns, unproven stack, high integration risk. Months of R&D needed.
technical_feasibility.5: Feasible but requires significant refactors, new dependencies, or expertise gaps.
technical_feasibility.7: Feasible with known trade-offs. No blocking unknowns. Existing patterns apply.
technical_feasibility.9: Mostly implemented or trivially achievable with current stack and team.
technical_feasibility.10: Already built. Just needs wiring, configuration, or UI integration.

financial_viability.1: Negative unit economics with no path to profitability.
financial_viability.3: Unit economics unclear. High burn, long payback, unproven revenue model.
financial_viability.5: Break-even achievable but timeline uncertain. Revenue model plausible.
financial_viability.7: Positive unit economics with reasonable assumptions. Break-even within 12-18 months.
financial_viability.9: Strong unit economics. Quick payback. Revenue model validated by competitors.
financial_viability.10: Proven revenue model with existing paying customers or pre-sales.

strategic_alignment.1: Contradicts current strategy. Dilutes focus. No defensible position.
strategic_alignment.3: Tangential to strategy. Weak moat. Easily replicated by competitors.
strategic_alignment.5: Aligned but not differentiating. Incremental improvement, not strategic advantage.
strategic_alignment.7: Strengthens core strategy. Builds defensible position. Clear competitive advantage.
strategic_alignment.9: Directly builds moat. Creates network effects, switching costs, or data advantages.
strategic_alignment.10: Transformative strategic move. Redefines competitive position in the market.

domain_fit.1: Violates fundamental industry constraints. Regulatory non-starter.
domain_fit.3: Significant domain gaps. Missing table-stakes features. Regulatory risks unaddressed.
domain_fit.5: Meets basic domain requirements but lacks industry depth. Generic approach.
domain_fit.7: Solid domain understanding. Table-stakes covered. Key regulations addressed.
domain_fit.9: Deep domain fit. Industry-specific features differentiate. Compliance built-in.
domain_fit.10: Industry-native solution. Exceeds domain requirements. Competitive advantage from domain expertise.
